title: centaurea drabifolia subsp. floccosa
intro: Centaurea drabifolia subsp. floccosa, also known as the Woolly Knob Cabbage or Woolly Knob Centaury, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its woolly leaves and small, white or pinkish flowers. Traditionally, Centaurea drabifolia subsp. floccosa has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Centaurea drabifolia subsp. floccosa can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions with other medications.
